description: Extensibility seam for the Ono Project Inspector plugin. The orchestrating agent (agents/project-inspector.md) reads this file to decide which skills exist, in what order, and how they connect to hooks. Adding a new skill never requires editing the agent, the command, or existing hooks — only this file plus the new skill folder (and optionally a new hook).

skills.notes: First stage. Creates the project's baseline AI context and the audit topic index. Must run before any other stage. Its CLAUDE.md template embeds the audit-sync managed-block markers that stage 4 later populates.

Second stage. Builds the docs/project/ knowledge base (descriptive: what exists and how things are done) on top of the CLAUDE.md context. Read-only over source. Complements audit-breakdown, which produces evaluative findings rather than a descriptive inventory.
Processes exactly one Pending Breakdown topic per invocation. The agent loops back to this skill only when the developer explicitly approves continuing to the next topic.

Final stage. Marks developer-approved topics as Approved in AUDIT.md and regenerates the two audit-sync managed blocks inside CLAUDE.md from ALL currently-Approved topics. Only acts on topics the developer explicitly names as approved — never decides approval itself. Re-runs replace the managed blocks rather than duplicating them, so it is safe to run repeatedly as more topics are approved.
